threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest Tube, while everyone else knows the best bus routes avoid the crowds.
Last night on the 59, a lad serenaded us with off-key ballads about lost loves. In Brixton, you never know if it’s karaoke night or just a regular bus ride.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a guy playing accordion next to a kebab stand while a bunch of kids danced in the rain. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you enjoy a 3 am currywurst while debating late-night politics with a punk on the corner. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they know to pack a picnic for Dolores Park, not just grab takeout and call it a day.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo sharing a Muni seat with a dog in a tutu. Only in SF do you get that kind of public transport fashion show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume Gràcia is just about Gaudí. The real magic is in the hidden plaça and old-school taverns that they skip right over.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by how they hold their phone up in the air while trying to get a selfie with the Casa Batlló. Locals just enjoy it quietly.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think asking for a 'cappuccino' will get you what you want at the café. It's all about the flat white here.
Last night on the 35, a guy loudly arguing with the driver about the 'best' chip shop in Brixton. Turned into an impromptu debate among passengers.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Caught a bus this morning and everyone just laughed when it broke down. That kind of shared moment makes you realize you’re really part of it all.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln can you enjoy a döner kebab at midnight while listening to live jazz on the street. It’s a real slice of Berlin life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who’s local at the BART station by whether they roll their eyes at the announcements about delays, or if they just shrug and text through it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a saxophone playing 'Take Five' on the 19. Strangely, the whole bus went quiet to listen. Just another day in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can avoid siesta, but you’ll find locals tucked in cafes, napping. That’s the time we recharge for evening tapas!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their map obsession, trying to find the fastest route to places everyone knows are just around the corner.
